div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Japan (Grew)
div.p: 233. Chungking’s 300, June 26, 8 p.m., and 302, June 27, 4 p.m., bombing of Chungking and 303, June 28, 7 p.m.None printed. The Department authorizes you, in your discretion and as occasion and appropriate opportunity therefor occur, to continue to bring to the attention of appropriate officers of the Japanese Foreign Office this Government’s attitude as repeatedly expressed, both upon broad humanitarian grounds and upon the ground of danger to American life and property, toward bombings of civilian populations such as those reported in the telegrams under reference.
Sent to Tokyo via Peiping. Repeated to Chungking and Tokyo.
